es.exampleTranslation: Better to talk it over in person.

it.exampleTranslation: Better to discuss it in person.

bridge.note: Spanish en persona does two jobs; Italian splits them — di persona for showing up yourself, in persona for 'the very man himself'. The Spanish speaker's import lands on the emphatic one by accident.

interference.es_to_it: EN pulls 'in persona' — which exists in Italian but leans emphatic ('the director himself'); the plain 'face to face' sense is di persona.
interference.it_to_es: DI pulls 'de persona' — not Spanish. En persona covers both jobs.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm. Cue drafted by Claude 2026-07-20 from the entry's meaning and bridge — English situational framing, not a language claim, so it needs editorial taste rather than native review. Override freely.

cue: How do you say you'll do it face to face?
